lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Stop! I can't help you unless you help me out first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then, moving on...

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: All is lost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Don't bother, you're not experienced enough to help me!

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Just look around you! This great building will soon be in ruin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great building?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Of course! This building has a history spanning generations!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see, a great history. But why will it be in ruin?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It's to be ripped apart to make way for King Lathas' new alchemists' lab.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the king not respect the outpost's GREAT history?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well, actually nobody knows what the history is. But there must be something.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Wait a minute. You could help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Yes! Uncover the history of this building to convince King Lathas to leave it alone. Do you want to know more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more.

lines.speaker: Narration
lines.text: With many occupants over the years...

lines.speaker: Narration
lines.text: ...the building has seen much action.

lines.speaker: Narration
lines.text: It started life as an outpost...

lines.speaker: Narration
lines.text: ... its sole purpose being to see invading armies...

lines.speaker: Narration
lines.text: ... before they saw the city of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: If all goes well, I hope to be able to turn it into a museum as a monument to the area's history. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, I will make a stand for history!

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Oh thank you so much, you really are my saviour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where should I start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: There are three people that may be able to help:

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: There is a silver trader in East Ardougne called Erin, who I believe can help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In what way can he help?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: His Great Grandfather lived in this outpost according to the records. He must know something!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, I'll see what he has to say.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: I've been told that there's a ghost far off in Port Phasmatys that moans of losing his life to this place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds ominous. Does he have a name?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: He does indeed. It's Droalak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll track him down.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It might not be so simple! You'll need an amulet of ghostspeak to talk to him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've conversed with the dead before, that shouldn't be too much of a problem.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Up near the mountains, in Rellekka is a warrior called Dron whom I have spoken to in the past. He is always on the lookout for information that can improve his fighting and commanding skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's he related to this outpost?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: He isn't directly, but he's studied many wars, and as this used to be an outpost it should have been involved in some war.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds simple enough.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: He isn't the easiest person to talk to, so you may need to speak to his brother, Blanin, first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the story so far?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: What do you mean? You've discovered nothing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got to go, bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't care about some dusty building.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It's doomed. DOOMED!

sections.title: Talking to Jorral again after getting the information

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Silver! Silver! Best prices for buying and selling in all Kandarin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found anything yet.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Keep trying. Have you noticed that the key changes temperature as you walk around?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: You're the one who stole something from me!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Guards guards!

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Silver! Silver! Best prices for buying and selling in all Kandarin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Erin?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: That I am. What do you want? You realise I'm working here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This shouldn't take too long. I just want to ask you a bit about your great Grandfather, the one who lived in the outpost?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm doing some research for a man called Jorral. Apparently the King is going to make the outpost into his very own alchemists' lab.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: That can only cause chaos! Well, my great Grandfather lived and died there according to my mother, but even she knows very little about him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: The only thing I have of his is a key. It's a strange key, it changes temperature by itself as you walk around. I'm afraid I don't know what it's for though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No idea at all?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Well, I imagine it's to some sort of chest of his belongings as it's too small for a door. Perhaps if you find some of his belongings you will discover some clues amongst them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's better than nothing. Will you lend it to me then?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Why not. I can't use it anyway. Try feeling its change in temperature as you walk around.

sections.title: Interacting with the key for the first time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I found a chest.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Best you look inside then.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Silver! Silver! Best prices for buying and selling in all Kandarin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like to see the journal I have found?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Very interesting. Best you show it to Jorral at the outpost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just about to. Do you want the key back?

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Keep it, it may come in handy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a journal!

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Good work. Let's see what it says.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: The person who wrote the journal spent time living in the outpost, following the order of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: He talks of all the nasty things they did to the people of Ardougne, which I don't care to mention! It looks like they were stopped by someone. But it doesn't say who.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: wooo wooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: Oh why did he leave me? Did he truly love me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm. I think you're talking about Droalak. I believe he did love you and he's very sorry for leaving you!

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: You're giving me empty words. That is all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you don't mind me asking, are you Melina?

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: That I am. What's it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been talking to Droalak. I believe he left you but never returned.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: He did. I suppose he has asked you to tell me he's sorry. What an empty gesture!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually he told me to give you this amulet.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: A sapphire amulet! He remembers! It's just like the one he gave me before he left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I honestly believe he's sorry.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: I'm so glad. Please, tell him I forgive him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will.

lines.speaker: Melina
lines.text: At last I feel complete. Fare well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: wooo wooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've given her the amulet. She was very pleased and said she just wanted to know you still cared.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Excellent! I am so glad she believes me. I can finally rest in peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have that scroll you mentioned first?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Of course. Let me know if it was of any use and then I can be forever free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank's for the scroll, but I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: It's a good job I stuck around then isn't it! Have another copy.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Take that scroll to Jorral in the outpost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been in contact with the ghost you suggested, and I've recovered this:

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Very interesting. So there was a great battle at the outpost. Then one of the survivors became king, and the other started the market place. Good work.

lines.speaker: Droklak
lines.text: wooo wooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Are you Droalak?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Wow. I haven't spoken to the living for... for... I don't remember how long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So your name IS Droalak?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Sorry, yes I am he.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Do you know anything about the outpost North of Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: I don't really like to talk about it, but I died there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: I do have a scroll which might interest you that describes the timeline of the outpost. But first I wonder if I could ask you to tie up a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Well, I left to go to the outpost against the wishes of my wife. I promised I would return to her, but obviously I did not as I died there. She's a ghost nearby, but won't listen to my apologies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to patch things up?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Yes, how'd you guess?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Call it 'traveller's intuition'.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: OK, well perhaps you could give her a strung sapphire amulet, because this is what I gave her the day I left. Her name is Melina by the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a sapphire amulet!

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: I said it needed to be a STRUNG sapphire amulet - best get some string!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a sapphire amulet!

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Good work. Just give it to Melina, who's wandering somewhere nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Make a strung sapphire amulet and give it to Melina!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Ok.

lines.speaker: Droklak
lines.text: wooo wooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Are you the brother of Dron?

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: That I am. Why? Has he killed one of your family?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not that I know of...

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: Oh good, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd like to talk to your brother Dron about the outpost North of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: I'm afraid he's not easy to talk to, so it's good that you came to see me. You'll need to remember a few things when talking to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like?

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: You must be firm with him and don't mention that I sent you. In case he asks, he wields an iron mace in battle, eats rats for breakfast, kittens for lunch and bunnies for tea!

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: His favorite drink is red spider blood, he's 36 years, 8 months and 21 days old, studies famous battles of the Fourth and Fifth ages, lives on the North East side of town and his, erm... pet cat is called Fluffy.

lines.speaker: Blanin
lines.text: I know this sounds strange but Dron won't talk to anyone unless they know him well - he's a secretive guy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Why should I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, sorry I don't mean to cause offence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please don't hurt me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm after important answers.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: But how do you know me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just talked to your brother.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Don't talk of my brother. He's not on my good side at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, you're the famous warrior Dron!

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: If so, what weapon do I use?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An iron mace.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: When do I like to eat rats?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Breakfast.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: When are kittens best devoured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lunch.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What do I usually eat for tea?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bunnies.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What colour spider blood tastes the best?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Red.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: How many years old am I?

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: and months?

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What are the most interesting ages for battles?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fifth and Fourth.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: And my house is situated where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: North East side of town.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What is my brother's name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Blanin.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: And my pet cat is called?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fluffy.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What's 5 plus 7?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 12, but what does that have to do with anything?

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Everything! Besides, it's 13!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I think you'll find it's 12.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Very well, you seem to know me quite well, I'll answer your questions best I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew!

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Well what are they then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to find out the history of the outpost near Ardougne. I was hoping you could help me.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Let me see. Ah yes, I remember reading of a battle that took place at the outpost many years ago. Two ex- friends led forces in a small battle that ended with them pitted against each other at the top of the outpost as the

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: sole survivors because of their superior strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ex-friends?

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: They were once friends but a difference in their beliefs meant they fell out. One chose to follow the god Zamorak, whilst the other chose Saradomin. But finding themselves in this extreme situation at the top of the

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: outpost caused them to see the errors of their ways, like the waste of life, the lost friendship and the wasted time. They both then decided to unite under Guthix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I like happy endings.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: You would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I can report this back to Jorral now.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was wondering how you get the blood stains out of your clothes?

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: BE GONE!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dave.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Dev?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. D-A-V-E

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: What a strange name. Unlike my brothers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kittens.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Eww. Gross. You have some strange tastes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't lecture ME on eating habits.

sections.title: If the player chooses the wrong option

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Don't waste my time!

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: You know nothing!

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Wrong, wrong, wrong!

sections.title: Talking to Jorral after receiving information from Dron

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've talked to the warrior.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Excellent! What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then I asked him about getting the blood stains out of his clothes, but he didn't like that!

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It seems you're lucky he didn't beat you up! Well done, that's another piece of the puzzle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Dron
lines.text: Look, I don't have time for weaklings, if you want conversation, talk to my brother Blanin!

sections.title: After talking to the last person

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Have you taken that letter to King Lathas in Ardougne yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Any time soon would be great.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: It all makes sense now, I never realised there was quite so much history to this place, it was more than I could have hoped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad to hear. What's the whole story?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Many years ago, there were two friends who fell out over their difference of opinion in religion. One decided to dedicate his life to Saradomin, the other to Zamorak. Years passed and the follower of Zamorak moved into

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: this outpost with a group of others to cause havoc for the city of Ardougne. The people of the city called upon some Saradomin followers, who happened to be led by the other friend. A battle ensued that ended with the

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: two ex-friends at the top of the outpost as the sole survivors. Realising their mistakes they made friends again. One decided to become King and spread the word of equality, and the other chose to start up a

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: market where all could trade their wares equally and fairly. The one that became king is the great grandfather of the King Lathas that we know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I do now?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well, what I'll do is write down the details you have provided in a more easy to digest manner, along with my plans for using this building as a museum.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Well I would give you the letter if you had space to carry it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I suppose you want ME to deliver it to the king?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: You've got that right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't suppose you could give me the letter again?

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Ok. Just don't lose it again!

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Have you taken that letter to Jorral yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me. I have been asked to give you from Jorral... Ooops, I had a letter, but I don't have it with me - be right back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me. I have been asked to hand you this from Jorral at the outpost.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I had no idea that place had any value at all! All this about my Great Grandfather and Jorral's plans to make it into a museum makes for a convincing case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am sure he only wants what is best.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Very well, I will comply with his request. Take this letter back to him with my kind regards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me sire, but I seem to have lost that letter you gave me.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Very well, take another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was given a letter to give to you from the king! But I seem to have lost it!

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Perhaps he will give you another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been to see the king and he gave me this letter.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Quick, let me see.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Hurrah! Good work, you've done it. You've saved the outpost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My pleasure.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: I can now continue with my plans for this place. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Jorral
lines.text: Take a look at the artefacts in the new museum room. You may find something of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have delivered the scroll, you can rest in peace now.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Thanks for telling me! I've been waiting for ages!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good bye.

lines.speaker: Droalak
lines.text: Bye!
